capstone_description: An interactive web app called the LLM Internals Playground. Panels include: live tokenizer, sampling distribution with temperature/top-p sliders, attention heatmap, embedding visualizer, hallucination risk indicator, model comparator. Built panel-by-panel across 10 lessons. Each lesson adds one working panel to the running app.

lessons.title: Exploring the Complete LLM Internals Playground
lessons.what_gets_built: A complete demo of the finished app runs locally, showing all panels working together with sample text.
lessons.codebase_state_after: Complete working demo app with all panels functional but code intentionally obfuscated/minified
lessons.why_here: Developers need to see the complete vision before committing time. This creates motivation and provides a concrete reference for what each subsequent lesson builds toward.
lessons.primary_question: What exactly am I going to build and why should I care?

lessons.title: Building a Live Tokenizer with Byte-Pair Encoding
lessons.what_gets_built: Users can type text and see it split into tokens in real-time with token IDs displayed.
lessons.codebase_state_before: Basic HTML/CSS structure with empty panel containers
lessons.codebase_state_after: Working tokenizer panel with input field, token display, and BPE implementation using tiktoken library
lessons.why_here: Tokenization is the first step in any LLM pipeline and must be understood before any other processing. It's concrete and immediately visual.
lessons.primary_question: How does an LLM convert my text into numbers it can actually work with?

lessons.title: Creating Word Embeddings with Vector Visualization
lessons.concept: Token embeddings as high-dimensional meaning representations
lessons.what_gets_built: Tokens from the tokenizer now display as 3D plotted vectors showing semantic similarity between words.
lessons.codebase_state_after: Embedding panel with Three.js visualization, vector similarity calculations, and connection to tokenizer output
lessons.codebase_state_before: Tokenizer panel working, empty embedding panel container
lessons.why_here: Once we have tokens, we need to understand how they become meaningful vectors. This builds directly on tokenization and enables understanding attention.
lessons.primary_question: How do token numbers become meaningful representations that capture word relationships?

lessons.title: Implementing Attention Mechanisms with Interactive Heatmaps
lessons.what_gets_built: A clickable heatmap shows which tokens the model pays attention to when processing each word in the input.
lessons.codebase_state_before: Tokenizer and embedding panels working, empty attention panel
lessons.codebase_state_after: Attention heatmap panel with matrix calculations, interactive hover effects, and attention weight visualization
lessons.why_here: Attention is the core mechanism of transformers and requires embeddings to be meaningful. This is the most complex concept so it gets dedicated focus.
lessons.primary_question: How does the model decide which words to focus on when generating each new word?

lessons.title: Building a Sampling Distribution Visualizer
lessons.what_gets_built: A bar chart shows probability distributions for next tokens, updating as users adjust temperature and top-p sliders.
lessons.codebase_state_before: Tokenizer, embedding, and attention panels working, empty sampling panel
lessons.codebase_state_after: Interactive sampling panel with probability bars, temperature/top-p controls, and real-time distribution updates
lessons.why_here: Now that we understand how models process input, we need to see how they generate output. This explains the randomness in LLM responses.
lessons.primary_question: Why does the same prompt give different responses, and how can I control that randomness?

lessons.title: Creating a Hallucination Risk Detection System
lessons.what_gets_built: A risk meter analyzes the probability distributions and flags when the model is likely hallucinating or uncertain.
lessons.codebase_state_before: All previous panels working, empty hallucination indicator space
lessons.codebase_state_after: Hallucination risk panel with entropy calculations, confidence scoring, and visual risk indicators
lessons.why_here: This builds directly on sampling distributions to show practical applications of probability analysis. It addresses a key developer concern about LLM reliability.
lessons.primary_question: How can I tell when an LLM is making stuff up versus giving reliable information?

lessons.title: Implementing Multi-Model Comparison Dashboard
lessons.concept: Model architecture differences through comparative analysis
lessons.what_gets_built: Users can switch between different models (GPT, Claude, Llama) and see how their internal processing differs for the same input.
lessons.codebase_state_before: Single-model version of all panels working
lessons.codebase_state_after: Model selector dropdown, API integrations for multiple models, side-by-side comparison views for all panels
lessons.why_here: Once learners understand one model deeply, comparing multiple models reinforces the concepts and shows practical differences developers encounter.
lessons.primary_question: How do different LLMs actually differ in their internal processing, and when should I use which one?

lessons.title: Adding Real-Time Performance Monitoring
lessons.concept: LLM performance metrics and bottleneck identification
lessons.what_gets_built: Performance graphs show token processing speed, memory usage, and computational costs across different models and inputs.
lessons.codebase_state_before: Multi-model comparison working without performance tracking
lessons.codebase_state_after: Performance monitoring panel with charts for latency, throughput, memory usage, and cost estimation
lessons.why_here: Performance is crucial for production use but requires understanding the full pipeline first. This addresses practical deployment concerns.
lessons.primary_question: How do I measure and optimize LLM performance for production applications?

lessons.title: Building Interactive Prompt Engineering Tools
lessons.concept: Prompt optimization through internal state analysis
lessons.what_gets_built: Users can experiment with prompt variations and see how changes affect attention patterns, confidence scores, and output quality.
lessons.codebase_state_before: All monitoring and comparison features working with fixed prompts
lessons.codebase_state_after: Prompt engineering panel with A/B testing interface, prompt templates, and effectiveness scoring
lessons.why_here: This synthesizes all previous learning into practical prompt engineering skills. It requires understanding of attention, confidence, and model differences.
lessons.primary_question: How can I systematically improve my prompts using what I know about LLM internals?

lessons.title: Deploying and Optimizing the Complete LLM Playground
lessons.concept: Production deployment and user experience optimization
lessons.what_gets_built: The app is deployed with proper error handling, loading states, responsive design, and sharing capabilities for demo purposes.
lessons.codebase_state_before: Fully functional app running locally with all panels complete
lessons.codebase_state_after: Production-ready deployed app with error handling, optimization, testing suite, and documentation
lessons.why_here: The final lesson focuses on shipping and polish rather than new concepts. This gives learners a portfolio piece and deployment experience.
lessons.primary_question: How do I turn this learning project into a polished demo I can show to others?
